---
title: |
  <img src="../_assets/ahri_logo.png" alt="AHRI logo" class="title-logo" />
  <br />
  CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
subtitle: "Data Linkage for Longitudinal Dataset"
author:
  - name: CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
    affiliation: Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)
    city: Durban & Somokhele
    country: South Africa
format:
  revealjs:
    controls: true
    progress: true
    slide-number: true
    css:
      - styles/presentation_0.css
      - styles/presentation_1.css
---

## Project Overview

- CO-LUMINATE links individual mental health outcomes from cohort studies to AHRI HDSS household trajectories.
- Main focus is longitudinal relationships between social determinants and depressive symptoms in youth aged 13-24.
- Linked sample size: **5,279** adolescents and young people.

## Study Question

- How do social stress exposures relate to depressed mood through time?
- Priority social determinants:
  - Socioeconomic status (SES)
  - Caregiver co-residency and parental absence
  - Household composition and crowding
- Outcomes use rigorous depression measures from cohort studies (SSQ-14 and PHQ-9).

## Data Linkage Design

- Start from individual-level cohort participants nested in AHRI HDSS.
- Link each participant to longitudinal household records across multiple waves.
- Derive aligned exposure windows for:
  - Early childhood (0-5)
  - Middle childhood (6-12)
  - Entire childhood (0-12)

## Data Sources (Cohorts)

- DREAMS
- Multilevel
- Isisekelo Sempilo
- Treatment as Prevention (TasP)
- Thetha Nami

These cohorts provide depression outcomes that are not routinely available in HDSS-only data.

## Dataset Outputs

- Harmonized participant-level analysis datasets for mental health and social exposures.
- Derived household social stress variables and documentation/codebook-ready metadata.
- Reproducible preparation pipeline for downstream statistical analysis objectives.

## Novel Contribution

- Integrates rigorous depression outcomes with long-run HDSS social exposure histories.
- Enables testing of **direct and mediation pathways** between social determinants and youth depression.
- Supports context-grounded interpretation in a low- and middle-income country setting.

## Co-Creation Component

- The project includes **11 Youth Co-Creators (YCC)**.
- YCC input is used to refine interpretation, framing, and relevance of analyses.
- Co-creation and secondary data analysis are integrated into one evidence-generation process.

## Thank You

- CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
- Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)
